cut a release

add a publish workflow that uploads to PyPI on v* tags, with a manual approval before upload
We need a proper release process for this library — versioning, changelog, tagging, the works.
set up pyproject.toml metadata and pick a build backend for this library
the wheel build fails with a hatchling metadata error
switch this project to uv with a src layout
add a github actions workflow that runs lint and tests on every PR
pin the actions in our test workflow to commit SHAs and make the checks required

Set up automated PyPI releases for my uv-managed package with trusted publishing
expected_behavior: asks about or determines the versioning strategy (static pyproject version vs git-tag-derived) and states the trade-off instead of silently picking one
walks through registering the trusted publisher on PyPI with the exact owner, repo, workflow filename, and environment fields
writes a tag-triggered publish workflow with id-token write and contents read permissions, a gated environment, and actions pinned to full commit SHAs
includes a uv lock refresh (uv lock --upgrade-package) committed together with the version bump so uv.lock never desyncs
keeps a human gate before upload (environment approval or PR merge) rather than publishing on every push to main

cases.prompt: PyPI publish fails with invalid-publisher / unable to authenticate even though I configured a trusted publisher
expected_behavior: checks the trusted publisher fields on PyPI character-by-character against the repo (owner and repo casing, workflow filename including the .yml extension, environment name)
checks whether the publish step runs via workflow_call or a reusable workflow, which breaks the exact-match validation
checks that the job runs on a GitHub-hosted runner, since self-hosted runners are not supported for trusted publishing
does not suggest falling back to a long-lived API token as the fix

cases.prompt: python-semantic-release bumped the version but the next uv build step fails in CI
expected_behavior: diagnoses the failure as uv.lock desync — the bump rewrote pyproject.toml but uv.lock still records the old version
adds uv lock --upgrade-package <package> between the bump and the build (for PSR, in build_command)
ensures the refreshed uv.lock is committed with the version-bump commit, not just regenerated in the runner
verifies checkout uses fetch-depth 0 so PSR can compute the bump from full history

cases.prompt: I want releases automated but a human must approve before anything reaches PyPI
expected_behavior: offers a PR-gated flow (release-please) or a gated GitHub environment with required reviewers as the approval mechanism
warns that tags and PRs created with the default GITHUB_TOKEN do not trigger downstream workflows, and shows the PAT or GitHub App workaround
notes environment required-reviewer protection is not available on private repos on lower GitHub plans
keeps the publish job permissions minimal (id-token write, contents read)

cases.prompt: generate a changelog for releases — should I just scrape it from commit messages?
expected_behavior: presents both commit-scraped changelogs (python-semantic-release, git-cliff) and human-written styles (Keep a Changelog, changesets-style notes), including the practitioner criticism that raw commit dumps are unhelpful to end users
recommends a concrete default (an Unreleased section rolled into each release, or curated notes reviewed on the release PR) instead of leaving the choice open
if commit scraping is chosen, ties it to conventional-commit discipline and notes that non-conforming commits cause wrong or missing bumps
keeps the version string free of extra text such as dates so downstream semver parsers do not break
